data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'truck driver jobs' and 'truck driver salary'

behavior: Spikes in truck driver job and wage searches signal labor market tightening in freight, often preceding freight rate increases and logistics sector rallies.

market_impact.rationale: Tight trucking labor markets correlate with stronger freight demand and pricing power for logistics and industrial companies.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'truck driver jobs' or 'truck driver salary' exceeds its 12-week median by 40% or more
trade_logic.exit: Close after 3 weeks or when index falls below 20% above median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor shortages in transportation are chronic; wage/job search spikes occur 4–6 times per year as seasonal demand fluctuates.

idea: Manufacturing Input Cost Pressure FRED Acceleration

data.source: FRED series PPI for intermediate materials (PPIICMM) weekly aggregation

behavior: Week-over-week acceleration in PPI for inputs signals rising cost pressures, which trigger equity rotation out of materials and into defensive sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Materials companies face margin compression when input costs accelerate faster than they can pass through prices.

trade_logic.entry: If 2-week change in PPI intermediate materials exceeds the 26-week rolling average change by 0.3% or more
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when acceleration reverses below median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED PPI data releases weekly; commodity and energy volatility produce input cost swings frequently enough to trigger monthly.

data.source: OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region weekly snapshot

behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in public EV charger deployment signals infrastructure capex cycles and local clean energy investment momentum.

market_impact.rationale: EV charging buildout drives industrial capex, construction jobs, and equipment orders for utilities and industrial suppliers.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly EV charger additions exceed 52-week median by 50% or more in any 3-state cluster
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if weekly additions fall back to median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional infrastructure grants and utility capex cycles create recurring deployment bursts; statistically fires 2–3 times per quarter.

idea: Airline Load Factor Collapse Fear Trade

data.source: BTS airline load factor and capacity data weekly aggregation

behavior: Sharp 2+ week drop in airline load factors signals demand weakness and signals bearish travel/discretionary sentiment.

market_impact.rationale: Airline load factor collapse is a leading indicator of consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending weakness.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly airline load factor drops 2+ points below its 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when load factor rebounds above 8-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings and economic uncertainty produce 3–4 load factor dips per year of 2+ points.

idea: Unemployment Claims Spike Jobless Fear Burst

data.source: FRED initial jobless claims (ICSA) daily/weekly release

behavior: Week-over-week jump in jobless claims exceeding seasonal adjustment by 15% or more signals labor market stress and consumer anxiety.

market_impact.rationale: Jobless claims spikes correlate with consumer pullback on discretionary spending and equity de-risking.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly jobless claims spike >15% above prior week and exceed 52-week median by 20%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when claims revert to median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Jobless claims volatility is weekly and frequent; threshold fires 6–10 times per year during normal volatility.

data.source: RSS/news feed count for 'supply chain bottleneck' and 'commodity shortage' articles via rss_count adapter

behavior: Daily spike in supply chain and commodity shortage news mentions signals market anxiety about logistics constraints and inflation.

market_impact.rationale: Shortage narratives boost commodity and materials equity valuations as investors price in scarcity premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If daily news article count for 'supply chain' OR 'commodity shortage' exceeds 90-day median by 60% or more
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when article count falls to median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Geopolitical events, weather, and logistics disruptions reliably produce supply chain news spikes weekly.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK, XLY, XLC relative to SPY

behavior: Multi-day surge in relative trading volume on mega-cap tech/growth stocks often coincides with corporate buyback windows; pullback signals mean reversion.

market_impact.rationale: Buyback-driven rallies are mechanical; when volume normalizes, momentum fades and reverses.

trade_logic.entry: If XLK or XLC volume exceeds 30-day median by 40% for 2 consecutive days AND price up >1%, short on day 3
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or if price rallies another 2%

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Corporate buyback windows occur quarterly; volume spikes happen 2–3 times per month during window periods.

idea: Weather Extreme Disruption Energy Demand Spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly by region + EIA electricity demand

behavior: Days with extreme heat/cold >2 std dev from normal drive electricity demand spikes and boost utility/energy pricing.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme weather drives peak electricity demand and margin expansion for utilities and power generators.

trade_logic.entry: If daily temperature anomaly >2 std dev from 20-year normal for region AND EIA electricity demand spike >8% from prior day
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 trading days or when temperature normalizes

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes produce 3–6 multi-day heat/cold waves annually; each fires signal reliably.

idea: Port Container Backlog Volatility Freight Rally

data.source: Port of LA/Long Beach container volume weekly data via port_container_volume adapter

behavior: Week-over-week swings in port container throughput >15% signal volatility in global trade flows and logistics demand.

market_impact.rationale: Port volatility creates urgency for shippers to use alternative routes/carriers; freight rates rise.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly port container volume swings >15% above or below 13-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when volatility normalizes to <8% of MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Container volume swings are seasonal and event-driven; fires 2–4 times per quarter.

idea: Earthquake Swarm Construction Material Demand Surge

data.source: USGS earthquake activity API for daily counts and magnitude by region

behavior: Multi-day earthquake swarms (>5 events in 48 hrs, magnitude 3.5+) in urban areas signal impending rebuilding demand and construction/materials activity.

market_impact.rationale: Post-earthquake reconstruction drives cement, steel, and materials demand.

trade_logic.entry: If daily earthquake count in a metro region >5 in 48 hours with avg magnitude >3.5
trade_logic.exit: Exit 10 trading days later or if swarm subsides for 5+ days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic swarms occur 2–4 times per month globally; US-based swarms fire 1–2 times per month.
